verses.2: “Therefore my disquieting thoughts make me respond, Even because of my inward agitation.
verses.3: “I listened to the reprimand which insults me, And the spirit of my understanding makes me answer.
verses.4: “Do you know this from ancient times, From the establishment of mankind on earth,
verses.5: That the rejoicing of the wicked is short, And the joy of the godless momentary?
verses.6: “Though his arrogance reaches the heavens, And his head touches the clouds,
verses.7: He perishes forever like his refuse; Those who have seen him will say, ‘Where is he?’
verses.8: “He flies away like a dream, and they cannot find him; Like a vision of the night he is chased away.
verses.9: “The eye which saw him sees him no longer, And his place no longer beholds him.
verses.10: “His sons favor the poor, And his hands give back his wealth.
verses.11: “His bones are full of his youthful strength, But it lies down with him in the dust.
verses.12: “Though evil tastes sweet in his mouth And he hides it under his tongue,
verses.13: Though he desires it and will not let it go, But holds it in his mouth,
verses.14: Yet his food in his stomach is changed To the venom of cobras within him.
verses.15: “He swallows riches, But will vomit them up; God will expel them from his belly.
verses.16: “He sucks the poison of cobras; The viper’s tongue kills him.
verses.17: “He does not look at the streams, The rivers flowing with honey and curds.
verses.18: “He returns the product of his labor And cannot swallow it; As to the riches of his trading, He cannot even enjoy them.
verses.19: “For he has oppressed and neglected the poor; He has seized a house which he has not built.
verses.20: “Because he knew no quiet within him, He does not retain anything he desires.
verses.21: “Nothing remains for him to devour, Therefore his prosperity does not endure.
verses.22: “In the fullness of his excess he will be cramped; The hand of everyone who suffers will come against him.
verses.23: “When he fills his belly, God will send His fierce anger on him And rain it on him while he is eating.
verses.24: “He may flee from the iron weapon, But the bronze bow will pierce him.
verses.25: “It is drawn and comes out of his back, Even the flashing point from his gallbladder; Terrors come upon him,
verses.26: Complete darkness is held in reserve for his treasures, And unfanned fire will devour him; It will consume the survivor in his tent.
verses.27: “The heavens will reveal his guilt, And the earth will rise up against him.
verses.28: “The increase of his house will disappear; His possessions will flow away on the day of His anger.
verses.29: “This is a wicked person’s portion from God, The inheritance decreed to him by God.”
